blocks:
Sūrat al-Munāfiqīn

Medinan by consensus, and it consists of eleven verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** إِذا جاءَكَ الْمُنافِقُونَ قالُوا نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللهِ وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُهُ وَاللهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ لَكاذِبُونَ ** (1) When the hypocrites come to you, they say, 'We bear witness that you are indeed the Apostle of Allah.' Allah knows that you are indeed His Apostle, and Allah bears witness that the hypocrites are indeed liars.

Because they did not believe that, since bearing witness (الشهادة) is an informing based on knowledge (علم). Because it (الشهادة) derives from الشهود (witnessing/presence), meaning presence (الحضور) and awareness (الاطّلاع). For this reason, He confirmed the thing witnessed (المشهود به) but declared them liars concerning the witnessing (الشهادة).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), Ṭāwūs al-Yamānī said to Him: "Inform me about a people who bore witness to the testimony of truth (شهادة الحقّ) yet were liars." He said: "The hypocrites (المنافقون) when they said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللهِ (We bear witness that you are indeed the Apostle of Allah)."

(2) اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (2) They have made their oaths a shield, and thus they bar [people] from the way of Allah. Evil indeed is what they have been doing. ** اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ ** Their false oaths (حلفهم الكاذب) ** جُنَّةً ** A shield (وقاية) against being killed and taken captive (عن القتل والسبي). ** فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** Barring (صدّاً) or turning away (صدوداً). إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ Due to their hypocrisy (نفاقهم) and their barring [others] (صدّهم).

(3) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا فَطُبِعَ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لا يَفْقَهُونَ ** (3) That is because they believed, and then they disbelieved. So a seal was set on their hearts, and thus they do not understand. ** ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا فَطُبِعَ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ ** Until they became accustomed (تمرّنوا) to disbelief (الكفر) and became firmly established (استحكموا) in it. ** فَهُمْ لا يَفْقَهُونَ ** The reality of faith (حقيقة الإيمان) nor do they know its validity (صحّته).

(4) وَإِذا رَأَيْتَهُمْ تُعْجِبُكَ أَجْسامُهُمْ وَإِنْ يَقُولُوا تَسْمَعْ لِقَوْلِهِمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ خُشُبٌ مُسَنَّدَةٌ يَحْسَبُونَ كُلَّ صَيْحَةٍ عَلَيْهِمْ هُمُ الْعَدُوُّ فَاحْذَرْهُمْ قاتَلَهُمُ اللهُ أَنَّى يُؤْفَكُونَ ** (4) When you see them, their bodies impress you, and if they speak, you listen to their speech. They are like propped-up logs. They suppose every cry is against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May Allah assail them! How are they led astray? ** وَإِذا رَأَيْتَهُمْ تُعْجِبُكَ أَجْسامُهُمْ ** Due to their size (لضخامتها) and handsomeness (صباحتها). ** وَإِنْ يَقُولُوا تَسْمَعْ لِقَوْلِهِمْ ** Due to their eloquence (لذلاقتهم) and the sweetness of their speech (حلاوة كلامهم). كَأَنَّهُمْ خُشُبٌ مُسَنَّدَةٌ Leaning against the wall (الى الحائط), in that they are mere forms (اشباحاً) devoid of knowledge (العلم) and insight (النظر). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says they neither hear nor understand (لا يسمعون ولا يعقلون). ** يَحْسَبُونَ كُلَّ صَيْحَةٍ عَلَيْهِمْ ** Meaning, every calamity (واقعة) is against them (عليهم), due to their cowardice (لجبنهم) and suspicion (واتّهامهم). ** هُمُ الْعَدُوُّ ** A new sentence (استيناف). ** فَاحْذَرْهُمْ قاتَلَهُمُ اللهُ ** A supplication against them (دعاء عليهم). ** أَنَّى يُؤْفَكُونَ ** How are they turned away (يصرفون) from the truth (الحقّ)?

(٥) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعالَوْا يَسْتَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ رَسُولُ اللهِ لَوَّوْا رُؤُسَهُمْ (5) When they are told, 'Come, so that the Apostle of Allah may plead with Him for your forgiveness,' they twist their heads, they turned them in aversion (إعراضًا) and arrogance (استكبارًا) from that. وَرَأَيْتَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ ** they turn away from seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار) وَهُمْ مُسْتَكْبِرُونَ ** about offering an apology (الاعتذار).

(٦) سَواءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ لَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ لَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ ** (6) It is the same to them whether you plead for forgiveness for them or do not plead for forgiveness for them: Allah will never forgive them. due to their deep-rootedness in disbelief (الكفر). ** إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ ** Indeed Allah does not guide the transgressing people. those who have gone outside the realm (مظنّة) of being reformed (الاستصلاح) because of their immersion (لانهماكهم) in disbelief (الكفر) and hypocrisy (النفاق).

(٧) هُمُ الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ أي للأنصار لا تُنْفِقُوا عَلى مَنْ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللهِ حَتَّى يَنْفَضُّوا ** (7) They are the ones who say, 'Do not spend on those who are with the Apostle of Allah until they disperse.' meaning, to the Ansar (الأنصار). They mean the poor among the Muhajirun (المهاجرين). وَلِلَّهِ خَزائِنُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** Yet to Allah belong the treasuries of the heavens and the earth, in His Hand are the provisions (الأرزاق) and the distribution (القسم). وَلكِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ لا يَفْقَهُونَ ** but the hypocrites do not understand** that, due to their ignorance of Allah.

(8) ** يَقُولُونَ لَئِنْ رَجَعْنا إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ لَيُخْرِجَنَّ الْأَعَزُّ مِنْهَا الْأَذَلَّ وَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلكِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (8) They say, 'If we return to the city, the mightier will surely expel the abased from it.' But might belongs to Allah and His Apostle and the faithful, though the hypocrites do not know. due to the excess of their ignorance (جهلهم) and their delusion (غرورهم).

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Battle of al-Muray', which is the Battle of Banu al-Mustaliq, in the fifth year of the Hijrah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had gone out to it, and when He returned from it, He stopped at a well where the water was scarce. Anas ibn Sayyar, an ally of the Ansar, and Jahjah ibn Sa'id al-Ghifari, a hired worker (أجيراً) for Umar ibn al-Khattab, gathered at the well. Sayyar's bucket became entangled with Jahjah's bucket. Sayyar said, "My bucket!" and Jahjah said, "My bucket!" So Jahjah struck Sayyar's face with his hand, and blood flowed from it. Sayyar called out to the Khazraj, and Jahjah called out to Quraysh. The people took up arms, and sedition (الفتنة) was about to break out. Abdullah ibn Ubayy heard the call and asked, "What is this?" They informed him of the news. He became intensely angry, then said: "I was already averse to this journey to the first of the Arabs. I did not think I would remain alive long enough to hear something like this without having the power to change it." Then he turned to his companions and said: "This is what you have done! You settled them in your homes, you shared your wealth with them, you protected them with your lives, and you exposed your necks to be killed, making your women widowed and your children orphaned! If you had expelled them, they would have been dependents upon others." Then he said: **لَئِنْ رَجَعْنا إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ لَيُخْرِجَنَّ الْأَعَزُّ مِنْهَا الْأَذَلَ ** (If we return to the city, the mightier will surely expel the abased from it.)

Zayd ibn Arqam was among the people; he was a youth who had reached puberty (قد راهق). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was in the shade of a tree at midday (وقت الهاجرة), and with Him were some of His companions from the Muhajirun and the Ansar. Zayd came and informed Him of what Abdullah ibn Ubayy had said. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Perhaps you were mistaken, O youth?" He said: "No, by Allah, I was not mistaken." He said: "Perhaps you were angry with him?" He said: "No, by Allah, I was not angry with him." He said: "Perhaps he treated you foolishly (سفه عليك)?" He said: "No, by Allah." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Shaqran, His freed slave (مولاه): "Saddle the riding beast (احدج)." So he saddled His riding beast, and He mounted it. The people heard about this and said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would not travel at such a time." So the people traveled. Sa'd ibn Ubadah caught up with Him and said: "Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings." He replied: "And upon you be peace." Sa'd said: "You would not travel at such a time." He said: "Have you not heard a saying uttered by your companion?" They said: "And which companion do we have other than you, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Abdullah ibn Ubayy claimed that if he returns to Medina, the mightier will surely expel the abased from it." Sa'd said: "O Messenger of Allah, You and Your companions are the mightier, and he and his companions are the abased." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم spent the entire day without anyone speaking to Him. The Khazraj approached Abdullah ibn Ubayy, reproaching him. Abdullah swore that he had not said any of that. They said: "Then come with us to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم so that you may apologize to Him." He twisted his neck (لوّى عنقه). When night fell, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم traveled all night and all day, only stopping for prayer. The next day, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم stopped, and His companions stopped, having been exhausted (أمهدهم الأرض) by the sleeplessness (السّهر) that afflicted them. Abdullah ibn Ubayy came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and swore that he had not said that, and that he bore witness that there is no god but Allah and that You are the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and that Zayd had lied against him. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم accepted this from him. The Khazraj turned upon Zayd ibn Arqam, reviling him and saying to him: "You lied against Abdullah, our master."

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم departed, Zayd was with Him, saying: "O Allah, You surely know that I did not lie against Abdullah ibn Ubayy." He had traveled only a short distance when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was overcome by the state (البرحاء) that used to seize Him when the revelation descended upon Him. He became so heavy that His she-camel nearly knelt down (تبرك) from the weight of the revelation. Then the state passed from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and sweat was pouring from His forehead. Then He took Zayd ibn Arqam by the ear and lifted him from the saddle. Then He said: "O youth, your word is true, and your heart retained it. Allah has revealed a Quran concerning what you said." When it was revealed, He gathered His companions and recited Surat al-Munafiqun (The Hypocrites) to them, and Allah exposed Abdullah ibn Ubayy. Al-Qummi said: When Allah described them to His Messenger and made them known to Him, their tribesmen walked up to them and said: "You have been exposed, woe to you! Go to the Prophet of Allah so that He may seek forgiveness for you." But they twisted their heads and showed disdain (زهدوا) for seeking forgiveness.

And in a narration: The son of Abdullah ibn Ubayy came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, if You have resolved to kill him, then command me to be the one who brings his head to You. For by Allah, the Aws and the Khazraj know that I am the most dutiful of sons to my father. I fear that if You command someone else to kill him, my soul will not be content to look upon the killer of Abdullah, and I might kill a believer for a disbeliever and thus enter the Fire." The Messenger of Allah said: "Rather, We shall treat him well as long as he is with us."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Almighty, named those who did not follow His Messenger concerning the ولاية (guardianship) of His successor (وصيّه) as منافقين (hypocrites). And He made the one who denied the succession (وصيّة) of his Imamate (إمامته) like the one who denied Muhammad. And He revealed a Quran concerning this, saying: "O Muhammad, إِذا جاءَكَ الْمُنافِقُونَ ** (When the hypocrites come to you) concerning the ولاية of your successor, قالُوا نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللهِ وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُهُ وَاللهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ ** (they say, 'We bear witness that you are indeed the apostle of Allah.' And Allah knows that you are indeed His apostle, and Allah bears witness that the hypocrites) concerning the ولاية of Ali (peace be upon him) لَكاذِبُونَ اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ (are certainly liars. They have made their oaths a shield, and thus they bar [people] from the way of Allah). And the Way (السبيل) is the Successor (الوصيّ). إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا (Evil indeed is what they have been doing. That is because they believed) in your message (رسالتك) ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا ** (then they disbelieved) in the ولاية of your successor, فَطُبِعَ ** (so Allah has set a seal) عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لا يَفْقَهُونَ (on their hearts, so they do not understand). He says: They do not comprehend your prophethood (نبوّتك). وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ (And when they are told) 'Return to the ولاية of Ali, يَسْتَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ (the Prophet will plead for forgiveness for you) from your sins,' لَوَّوْا رُؤُسَهُمْ (they twist their heads). Allah said: وَرَأَيْتَهُمْ يَصْدُّونَ (and you see them barring [people]) from the ولاية of Ali (peace be upon him) وَهُمْ مُسْتَكْبِرُونَ ** (while they are arrogant) toward him. Then Allah shifted the discourse, based on His knowledge of them, and said: سَواءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ لَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ لَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ (It is all the same to them whether you plead for forgiveness for them or do not plead for forgiveness for them: Allah will never forgive them. Indeed, Allah does not guide the transgressing lot). He says: The oppressors (الظالمين) of your successor.

(٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُلْهِكُمْ أَمْوالُكُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُكُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (9) O you who have faith! Do not let your wealth distract you, nor your children, from the remembrance of Allah. Let not the management of them and the concern for them distract you from His remembrance, such as the prayer and the rest of the acts of worship. وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** And whoever does that—it is they who are the losers. because they sold the great, enduring thing for the contemptible, perishable thing.

(١٠) ** وَأَنْفِقُوا مِنْ ما رَزَقْناكُمْ ** (10) And spend out of what We have provided you some of your wealth, as a saving for the Hereafter, مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ ** before death overtakes any of you, that he sees its signs (دلائله), فَيَقُولَ رَبِّ لَوْ لا أَخَّرْتَنِي ** whereat he will say, 'My Lord, why did You not respite me, You granted me respite (أمهلتني), **إِلى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُنْ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ ** for a short term, so that I may give charity and be among the righteous?'

In al-Faqih: He was asked concerning the saying of Allah: فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُنْ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ ** (so that I may give charity and be among the righteous?). He said: "I give charity (أصدق) from the charity (الصدقة), وَأَكُنْ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ.** (and be among the righteous)."

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Righteousness (الصلاح) here means the Hajj (الحجّ)."

(١١) ** وَلَنْ يُؤَخِّرَ اللهُ نَفْساً إِذا جاءَ أَجَلُها. (11) And Allah will never respite any soul when its term has arrived. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Verily, with Allah are suspended books (كُتُباً مَوْقُوفَةً); He advances from them what He wills and delays what He wills. Then, when the Night of Decree (لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ) arrives, Allah sends down in it everything that will occur until the next one (Night of Decree). That is His saying: وَلَنْ يُؤَخِّرَ اللهُ نَفْساً إِذا جاءَ أَجَلُها (And Allah will never respite any soul when its term has arrived)—when Allah has sent it down and written it as the writing of the heavens (كِتَابَ السَّمَاوَاتِ), it is that which He does not delay. ** وَاللهُ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ ** (11) And Allah is well aware of what you do. And it was recited with the yā’ (يَاء) (i.e., يَعْمَلُونَ). The reward for reciting this sūrah has already preceded.
